scheme_name: Dr.Babasaheb Ambedkar Udyog Uday Yojana: Assistance in Implementation of Information and Communication Technology (ICT)
page_title: Dr.Babasaheb Ambedkar Udyog Uday Yojana: Assistance in Implementation of Information and Communication Technology (ICT)

details: The scheme "Dr.Babasaheb Ambedkar Udyog Uday Yojana" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state's dynamic MSME sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from August 7th, 2020 to August 6th, 2025, this initiative supports SC/ST entrepreneurs and strengthens MSMEs, making them globally competitive.

The component "Assistance in Implementation of Information and Communication Technology (ICT)" offers financial assistance to enterprises for the implementation of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) solutions, including hardware for cloud computing and networking.
benefits: Covers up to 75% of the capital expenditure for ICT facilities maximum upto ₹5,00,000/-
eligibility: The firm must be a Micro/Small/Medium enterprise run by SC/ST entrepreneur.
The scheme is available to all eligible enterprises that plan to implement ICT solutions, such as hardware for cloud computing and networking.
SC/ST Enterprise means the enterprise in which contribution of SC/ST entrepreneur is 51% or more.

online: portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form.
Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit".
Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID.
After verification, you will be registered successfully.
Enter the email address and password you used during registration.
Click the "Login" button to access your account.
Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.

documents_required: Copy of the Constitution of the enterprise.
CA certificate for fixed capital investment.
CA certified Detailed expenditure statement for obtaining the ICT System.
Undertaking for any assistance from GoG and Gol.
Copy of the bill of expenditure occurred.
Copy of GPCB approved NOC/Consent & other departments approval (if applicable).
Copy of PAN Card of Enterprise, promoters/promoters/Directors, and Authorized signatory.
Copy of Audited Balance sheet/ ITR of the last financial year.
Certified copy of Brief project report regarding ICT /cloud computing including benefit analysis.
Self-Attested documents/ details to be submitted by the Authorized Person.
Power of attorney/ Board resolution letter/consent letter.

faqs.question: What is the "Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Udyog Uday Yojana"?
faqs.answer: It is a special scheme introduced under the Industrial Policy 2020 to provide financial assistance and support to Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) entrepreneurs in the MSME sector.

faqs.question: What sectors are prioritized under the Industrial Policy 2020?
faqs.answer: The policy prioritizes industries such as Auto and Auto Components, Chemicals and Petrochemicals, Pharmaceuticals, Cement, Textiles, Engineering, Gems and Jewellery, and Ceramics.

faqs.question: How does the scheme promote export competitiveness?
faqs.answer: The scheme emphasizes "Vocal for Local to become Global," strengthening integrated value chains and encouraging MSMEs to enhance their export potential.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is effective from August 7, 2020, and will remain in operation until August 6, 2025.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the component "Assistance in Implementation of Information and Communication Technology (ICT)"?
faqs.answer: The component "Assistance in Implementation of Information and Communication Technology (ICT)" provides financial assistance for the implementation of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) infrastructure.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this component?
faqs.answer: Any SC/ST MSME unit that plans to implement ICT infrastructure and meets the eligibility criteria can apply for the scheme.

faqs.question: What percentage of the capital expenditure is covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides financial assistance covering up to 75% of the capital expenditure required for ICT facilities.

faqs.question: What is the maximum financial assistance that can be availed?
faqs.answer: The maximum financial assistance available under the scheme is ₹5,00,000/-.

faqs.question: What types of ICT equipment are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers ICT-related equipment, including communication devices, networking hardware, and cloud computing access tools.
